title: COMPAT — Constraint-Market Compatibility: Finfish Farming and Fish Hatcheries (NAICS 112511)

sections.title: Market Context
sections.content:
| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **NAICS** | 112511 |
| **Title** | Finfish Farming and Fish Hatcheries |
| **CFJ** | Cultivate finfish from egg or fingerling to market-size harvest in a controlled aquatic environment [SRC: VN_finfish_farming] |
| **Primary Use Case** | Flow monitoring in RAS main loop, individual tank supply, and temperature control circuits [SRC: VN_finfish_farming] |
| **L-Path** | L7 > L6.10 Water Quality Monitoring & Control > L5.10.1 Flow Measurement > L4.10.1.1 Ultrasonic Flow Sensor [SRC: VN_finfish_farming] |
| **Architecture Distance** | 4 (adjacent) [SRC: 05_market_discovery] |
| **Operating Medium** | Fresh or brackish water, 10–30 °C, 0.5–3 bar, DN15–DN50 piping [SRC: VN_finfish_farming] |

---

sections.title: Constraint Assessments
sections.content:
8 constraints from Step 01 assessed against this market. Verdicts: **0 knockout, 2 mitigable, 6 none.**

### Summary Table

| # | Constraint | Type | Threshold | Verdict | Rationale |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Media Limitation | physical | water-like media only | **none** | RAS circulates water — exact design medium |
| 2 | Maximum Medium Temperature | physical | 90 °C | **none** | RAS operates 10–30 °C; gap >3× |
| 3 | Maximum Operating Pressure | physical | 10 bar | **none** | RAS piping 0.5–3 bar; gap >3× |
| 4 | Minimum Flow Rate Detection | operational | 0.25–4.2 l/min | **none** | Tank-level feeds 5–30+ l/min; main loop much higher |
| 5 | Air Bubble Tolerance | physical | ~2% vol | **mitigable** | Aeration/oxygenation stages create bubbles; sensor must be downstream of degasser |
| 6 | Pipe Full Condition | operational | pipe must be full | **none** | RAS main loop is closed-pipe pressurized; always full |
| 7 | Minimum Straight Pipe Run | operational | 5–10× DN | **mitigable** | Compact RAS layouts may have tight piping; requires installation planning |
| 8 | Temperature-Dependent Accuracy | physical | ~1.5% uncompensated | **none** | RAS temperature stable ±1 °C; NTC compensation reduces error to ~0.2% |

---

### Detailed Assessments

#### 1. Media Limitation — none

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Constraint** | Only water and water-like media (incl. glycol, particulate suspensions). Cannot measure oils, gases, or non-polar liquids. |
| **Threshold** | Acoustic impedance match with water required |
| **Market Conditions** | RAS systems circulate water (fresh or brackish). Fish live in water. The medium IS water with dissolved salts, organic matter, and suspended particulates — all acoustically transparent. [SRC: VN_finfish_farming] |
| **Verdict** | **none** |
| **Rationale** | Water is the sensor's design medium. Particulate suspensions (fish waste, biofilm fragments) are tolerated per spec. No acoustic impedance issue exists. |

#### 2. Maximum Medium Temperature (90 °C) — none

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Constraint** | Polymer housing and seals degrade above 90 °C. |
| **Threshold** | 90 °C |
| **Market Conditions** | Coldwater species (salmon, trout): 10–16 °C. Warmwater species (tilapia, barramundi): 26–30 °C. Maximum rearing temperature never exceeds 32 °C. [SRC: VN_finfish_farming, web_search] |
| **Verdict** | **none** |
| **Rationale** | Maximum water temperature in any RAS configuration (32 °C) is 58 °C below the 90 °C limit. Gap >2.8× — no risk under any operating scenario. |

#### 3. Maximum Operating Pressure (10 bar) — none

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Constraint** | Housing rated to 10 bar continuous. |
| **Threshold** | 10 bar |
| **Market Conditions** | RAS main loops operate at 0.5–3 bar (centrifugal pump head). Even with water-hammer events during pump start/stop, pressures stay well below 10 bar. [SRC: VN_finfish_farming] |
| **Verdict** | **none** |
| **Rationale** | RAS piping pressures (0.5–3 bar) are 3–20× below the 10 bar limit. Water hammer resistance >16 bar provides additional margin. |

#### 4. Minimum Flow Rate Detection (0.25–4.2 l/min) — none

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Constraint** | Signal-to-noise ratio degrades at very low flow rates. Lower bound 0.25 l/min (DN14) to 4.2 l/min (DN20/DN25). |
| **Threshold** | 0.25–4.2 l/min (variant-dependent) |
| **Market Conditions** | RAS individual tank supply: 5–30 l/min (DN15–DN25). Main loop: 100–12,000 l/min depending on farm scale. Even the smallest fry/fingerling tanks in hatcheries circulate >5 l/min. [SRC: VN_finfish_farming] |
| **Verdict** | **none** |
| **Rationale** | Lowest RAS flow (small fingerling tank, ~5 l/min) exceeds the DN14 minimum (0.25 l/min) and the DN20 minimum (4.2 l/min). No measurement gap. |

#### 5. Air Bubble Tolerance (~2% vol) — mitigable

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Constraint** | Air entrainment >2% by volume attenuates ultrasonic signal and degrades accuracy. |
| **Threshold** | ~2% vol |
| **Market Conditions** | RAS includes active aeration (MBBR coarse-bubble diffusers), oxygenation (low-head oxygenators, oxygen cones), and CO2 stripping — all of which introduce gas into the water at specific process stages. Main circulation pipe downstream of degassers is typically bubble-free, but installation near aeration or oxygenation points could expose sensor to >2% air. [SRC: VN_finfish_farming] |
| **Verdict** | **mitigable** |
| **Mitigation Actions** | 1. Install sensor downstream of CO2 stripper / degasser where water is bubble-free. 2. Ensure pump suction design prevents vortex air entrainment. 3. Use sensor's built-in bubble detection diagnostic as early warning for abnormal air entrainment. |
| **Mitigation Cost** | low (<$10K) |
| **Mitigation Time** | 1–2 weeks (installation positioning guidance in technical documentation) |
| **Rationale** | RAS main loop water is degassed by design (CO2 strippers are standard in all RAS). The constraint is relevant only if the sensor is mis-installed near an aeration or oxygenation point. Correct positioning eliminates the issue. The sensor's Digital Anomaly Detection feature provides a built-in safety net. |

#### 6. Pipe Full Condition — none

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Constraint** | Pipe must be fully filled with liquid for valid measurement. Partially filled pipes cause complete signal loss. |
| **Threshold** | Pipe must be full |
| **Market Conditions** | RAS main loop is a closed, pressurized piping system. All pipe sections between treatment stages and rearing tanks are fully filled during normal operation. Open-channel sections (e.g., trickling tower sump, tank overflow weirs) exist but are not closed-pipe measurement points. [SRC: VN_finfish_farming] |
| **Verdict** | **none** |
| **Rationale** | All closed-pipe sections in the RAS loop are pressurized and fully filled. The sensor's empty-pipe detection provides an additional safety diagnostic for pump failure events. |

#### 7. Minimum Straight Pipe Run (5–10× DN) — mitigable

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Constraint** | Turbulent or asymmetric flow profiles from upstream elbows, valves, or tees degrade accuracy. Requires 5–10× DN straight pipe upstream. |
| **Threshold** | 5–10× DN (100–250 mm for DN20/DN25) |
| **Market Conditions** | RAS piping layouts vary from spacious (large commercial facilities) to compact (small hatcheries, containerized RAS units). Compact installations may have elbows and valves close to measurement points. [SRC: VN_finfish_farming] |
| **Verdict** | **mitigable** |
| **Mitigation Actions** | 1. Include straight-run requirement (100–250 mm upstream) in RAS piping design guidelines. 2. For retrofit into existing compact installations, add a flow conditioner (vane-type or tab-type) upstream of sensor. |
| **Mitigation Cost** | low (<$10K) |
| **Mitigation Time** | 1–2 weeks (design guideline for new builds; flow conditioner retrofit for existing) |
| **Rationale** | 100–250 mm of straight pipe is achievable in most RAS layouts. Compact or containerized systems may require a flow conditioner ($20–50 part) or minor pipe rerouting. This is a standard installation consideration for any inline flowmeter, not specific to this market. |

#### 8. Temperature-Dependent Accuracy — none

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Constraint** | Speed of sound changes with temperature. Uncompensated error ~1.5%; NTC compensation reduces to ~0.2%. Extreme transients may exceed compensation response. |
| **Threshold** | ~1.5% error uncompensated |
| **Market Conditions** | RAS temperature is actively controlled to species-specific setpoints with ±1 °C stability. Temperature changes are gradual (heat exchangers respond over minutes, not seconds). No extreme thermal transients. [SRC: VN_finfish_farming] |
| **Verdict** | **none** |
| **Rationale** | RAS temperature is the most tightly controlled parameter in the system (±1 °C). The built-in NTC compensation easily handles this range. The integrated temperature sensing provides additional value by co-reporting rearing water temperature. |

---

sections.title: Result
sections.content:
| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Knockouts** | 0 |
| **Mitigable** | 2 (Air Bubble Tolerance, Minimum Straight Pipe Run) |
| **None** | 6 |
| **Market Status** | **SURVIVING** — no knockouts; passes all constraints |
| **Total Mitigation Cost** | low (<$10K combined) |
| **Total Mitigation Time** | 1–2 weeks (installation documentation and design guidelines) |

**Assessment:** The Marquardt Ultrasonic Flow Sensor has excellent constraint compatibility with finfish farming / RAS. The product's design medium (water), operating range (2–90 °C, 0–10 bar), and pipe diameter family (DN14–DN25) are a natural fit for RAS water circulation monitoring. The two mitigable constraints (air bubble positioning, straight pipe run) are standard installation considerations solvable through application engineering documentation — no product redesign required.

---
